Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 323 [Šumma Alu 032][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] ⸢NA⸣ ŠUBut NA BI ul [x] x [...]

(o 1) [...] falls [...] a man: that man will not ... [...].

o 22

[...] UGU NA ŠUBut SUḪUR? ⸢IR ZIQ⸣ [...]

(o 2) [...] falls on a man: ... [...].

o 33

[*] ⸢EME⸣.[ŠID] [x] ⸢UGU⸣ bu-di [...] ŠUB-ma [...]

(o 3) (If) a lizard [...] falls over the shoulder of [...] and [...].

o 44

[*] EME.⸢ŠID⸣ ana IGI NA ⸢ŠUB⸣ut KURad EN DU₁₁-[šu₂] [...]

(o 4) (If) a lizard falls in front of a man: defeating his enemy.

o 55

[*] EME.ŠID ana IGI NA ⸢ŠUB⸣ut UGU EN DU₁₁-šu₂ GUB⸢az⸣

(o 5) (If) a lizard falls in front of a man: he will triumph over his enemy.

o 66

[*] EME.ŠID ana ⸢EGIR NA ŠUB⸣ut-⸢ma⸣ [...] SU pi₂-is-lat ḪUL IGI

(o 6) (If) a lizard falls behind a man and [...]: he will experience evil misadventures.

o 77

[*] EME.ŠID ana ⸢EGIR⸣ NA ŠUBut-ma [it]-ta-pi₂-iṣ MUNUS.ḪUL IGI

(o 7) (If) a lizard falls behind a man and gets crushed: he will experience evil.

o 88

[*] ⸢EME⸣.ŠID TA ⸢AN⸣e ana UGU [NA] ⸢ŠUB⸣ut ḪA.LA [...]

(o 8) (If) a lizard falls from the sky on top of a man: [...] profit.

o 99

* EME.ŠID TA GIŠGAL? ana UGU NA [ŠUB]ut NA BI ⸢IGI?⸣ [...]

(o 9) (If) a lizard falls from a socle on top of a man: that man ... [...].

o 1010

* EME.ŠID TA KI ana UGU NA [...] DU NIG₂.⸢TUK!⸣ [...]

(o 10) (If) a lizard [...] from the ground on top of a man: [...] riches.

o 1111

* EME.ŠID TA E₂.GAR₈ ana UGU NA x x MA x x A MEŠ? [...]

(o 11) (If) a lizard ... from a wall on top of a man: ... [...].

o 1212

* EME.⸢ŠID⸣ ana UGU ŠU NA ZA₃ [ŠUB]ut ⸢NIG₂⸣.TUK [...]

(o 12) (If) a lizard falls on top of a man's right hand: [...] riches.

o 1313

* ⸢EME.ŠID⸣ ana UGU ŠU NA GUB₃ ⸢ŠUB⸣ut ina ⸢KU₃⸣.BABBAR ⸢DUMU⸣.MUNUS-MEŠ-šu₂ E₂ [...]

(o 13) (If) a lizard falls on top of a man's left hand: he [...] a house with his daughters' silver.

o 1414

* ⸢EME⸣.[ŠID] [ana] UGU GIR₃ NA 15 ⸢ŠUB⸣ut mim-ma KU₃.BABBAR x EN.NUN.⸢U₄⸣.[ZAL.LA ...]

(o 14) (If) a lizard falls on top of a man's right foot: everything silver; ... dawn watch.

o 1515

[...] ana UGU GIR₃ NA 2.30 ⸢ŠUB⸣ut ḪULUḪ GIR₃ TUKU

(o 15) [...] falls on top of a man's left foot: he will be affected by trembling of the foot.

o 1616

[...] UGU GIR₃ NA ⸢E₁₁⸣ NA BI i-ḫad-[du]

(o 16) [...] climbs on top of a man's foot: that man will rejoice.

o 1717

[...] ⸢UGU⸣ NA ŠUBut x U₂ MU ⸢Aʾ⸣ U₄ šu-a-⸢tu₄⸣ [...]

(o 17) [...] falls on top of a man: ... .

o 1818

[...] ⸢UGU⸣ bu-di NA ŠUB⸢ut⸣ TAG-su ⸢INIM⸣ la₃ ZU KUR x [...]

(o 18) [...] falls on top of a man's shoulder (and) hurts him, he will win an unexpected lawsuit.

o 1919

[...] ⸢UGU gišGU.ZA NA ŠUB⸣ut x EN.NUN.U₄.ZAL!(ME)

(o 19) [...] falls on top of a man's chair: ... dawn watch.

o 2020

[...] ⸢UGU⸣ gišGU.[ZA] NA E₁₁ NA BI is-sal-⸢la-aʾ⸣

(o 20) [...] climbs up (on) top of a man's chair: that man will become infected.

o 2121

[...] TA gišGU.⸢ZA⸣ NA KU₄-ma ir-bi-iṣ E₂ BI TUŠab

(o 21) [...] enters under a man's chair and lies down: that house will be inhabited.

o 2222

[...] x giš⸢BANŠUR⸣ NA ⸢ŠUBut ḪAL?⸣ ina-an-ziq

(o 22) [...] falls (on) a man's table: ... he will become annoyed.

o 2323

[...] gišBANŠUR NA ana DUG.kal-li ŠUBut ⸢GIG?⸣ x DABsu

(o 23) [...] falls (on) a man's table, into a bowl: an illness ... will seize him.

o 2424

[...] ⸢giš⸣[...] E₁₁ ir-bi-iṣ ša₂ KASKAL ⸢GIG?⸣ TIšu₂

(o 24) [...] climbs up a wooden ... (and) lies down: an illness will take the one who is on a journey.

o 2525

[...] x NA KU₄-ma ir-bi-iṣ SUḪUŠ-BI NU GI.NA

(o 25) [...] enters ... of a man and lies down: his position will not be firm.

o 2626

[...] x ŠUBut ina-an-⸢ziq⸣

(o 26) [...] ... falls: he will become annoyed.

o 2727

[...] x E₁₁ INIM.GAR ⸢SIG₅⸣

(o 27) [..] climbs up ... [...]: (his) reputation will improve.

o 2828

[...] E₁₁ [...] DUMU-MEŠ ⸢NU⸣ IGI

(o 28) [...] climbs up [...]: he will not see (his) descendants.

o 2929

[...] x x [...] nu-kur₂ gišNU₂

(o 29) [...] ... [...]: demolishing of the bed.

o 3030

[* EME].⸢ŠID⸣ ana UGU [...] x MA ⸢ŠUB⸣[ut] NA BI ḪA.LA GU₇ x

(o 30) (If) a lizard falls on top of [...] ...: that man will enjoy a profit.

o 3131

[* EME].⸢ŠID⸣ ana KI.TA giš[...] ŠU MA NU₂iṣ ŠA₃-BI DU₁₀.GA

(o 31) (If) a lizard ... underneath a wooden [...] and lies down: he will be satisfied.

o 3232

[* EME].⸢ŠID⸣ ana!(ina) KI.TA giš[...] x DAḪ ḪA x nu-kur₂ gišNU₂

(o 32) (If) a lizard ... underneath a wooden [...]: demolishing of the bed.

o 3333

[* EME].⸢ŠID⸣ ana UGU gišNU₂ [...] ⸢ŠUB⸣ut GIG BI GIG-su ŠUBut

(o 33) (If) a lizard falls on top of a bed [...]: (his) illness will strike that patient.

o 3434

[* EME].⸢ŠID⸣ ana UGU gišNU₂ [...] x E₁₁ GIG BI GIG-su ŠUBut

(o 34) (If) a lizard climbs up on top of a bed [...] ...: an(his) illness will strike that patient.

o 3535

[* EME].⸢ŠID ana UGU NA⸣ ša₂ ⸢di⸣-[na i]-gi-ru-u ŠUBut NA BI ana EN di-ni-šu₂ TI

(o 35) (If) a lizard falls on top of a man who is initiating a lawsuit: that man will win against his adversary.

o 3636

[...] ŠA₂ x [...] aš-bu ŠUBut NA BI ina KI.ŠU₂ E₃

(o 36) [...] falls [...]: that man will escape from captivity.

o 3737

[...] ŠUBut E₃ NA BI ina PAP.ḪAL u KISLAḪ₃ ⸢E₃⸣

(o 37) [...] falls (and) goes out: that man will escape from restlessness and distress.

o 3838

[...] ŠUBut-ma ib-šal IDIM BA.⸢UŠ₂⸣

(o 38) [...] falls and burns up: a noblemen will die.

o 3939

[...] ⸢E₂⸣ BI ar₂!(NU-)bu-tu₂ DU⸢ak?⸣

(o 39) [...] that house will be laid waste.

Bottom
b.e. 1b.e. 1

[...] E₃ [...] x ⸢NA⸣ [...]

(b.e. 1) [...] goes out [...] ... man [...].

b.e. 22

[...] ma ib-⸢šal⸣ x BI x [...]

(b.e. 2) [...] ... and burns up ... that ... [...].

b.e. 33

[...] ma la₃ ib-šal ZI x [...]

(b.e. 3) [...] and does not burn up ... [...].

Reverse
r 1r 1

[...] ⸢ŠUB⸣ut NINDA I x x RA [...]

(r 1) [...] falls: food ... [...].

r 22

[...] ma ir-bi-iṣ NINDA i-⸢qa₂⸣-ar-⸢šu₂⸣

(r 2) [...] and lies down: food will become rare for him.

r 33

[...] ŠUBut DU₁₀.GA DINGIR GAL₂ši

(r 3) [...] falls: there will be divine goodness.

r 44

[...] ŠUBut NA BI ina-an-ziq

(r 4) [...] falls: that man will be annoyed.

r 55

[...] x x [...] x ŠUBut NA BI ina-an-ziq

(r 5) [...] falls: that man will be annoyed.

r 66

[* EME].ŠID ana dugnar₃-ṭa-bi ŠUBut NA BI maš-qa-a u₂-za-am-ma

(r 6) (If) a lizard falls into a narṭabu-vessel: that man will be deprived of a watering place.

r 77

[*] ⸢EME⸣.ŠID <ana> dugUTUL₂ ŠUBut GU₃ ina E₂ NA BI IGI

(r 7) (If) a lizard falls (into) a tureen: wailing will be experienced in that man's house.

r 88

[*] ⸢EME⸣.ŠID ana I₃.DUB DIRI ŠUBut ⸢IS ḪI⸣ I₃.DUB-MEŠ DIRI-MEŠ KU₄-MEŠ

(r 8) (If) a lizard falls into a spare storage vessel ...: ... will enter the spare storage vessels.

r 99

[*] ⸢EME⸣.ŠID ana I₃.DUB ⸢SUD?⸣ ŠUBut E₂ BI NINDA i-šeb-bi

(r 9) (If) a lizard falls into an empty storage vessel: that house will be full of food.

r 1010

[...] ana kušLU.⸢UB₂⸣ KU₄-ma ir-bi-iṣ NA BI ina-an-ziq

(r 10) (If) a lizard enters a leather bag and lies down: that man will be annoyed.

r 1111

[...] ana UGU ⸢NA⸣ x NA BI u₂-lab-bar

(r 11) [...] on top of a man: ... that man will live long.

r 1212

[...] ana UGU [...] ⸢ma⸣ i-du-lu NA BI ḪA.LA GU₇

(r 12) [...] on top of [...]... and it roams around: that man will enjoy profit.

r 1313

[...] ana ⸢UGU⸣ [...] U₄-MEŠ-šu₂ GID₂.⸢DA⸣-MEŠ

(r 13) [...] on top of [...]: his days will become long.

r 1414

[...] AK U₄-MEŠ-šu₂ GID₂.⸢DA⸣-[MEŠ]

(r 14) [...] ...: his days will become long.

r 1515

[...] ⸢ŠI⸣ ŠUBut ⸢ZI⸣.GA x [...]

(r 15) [...] ... falls: loss ... [...].

r 1616

[...] DU NA BI dLAMMA NIG₂.TUK ⸢TUKU?⸣

(r 16) [...] ... that man will acquire good fortune (and) riches.

r 1717

[...] x x ina E₂ NA GAL₂ši e-nešneš₂ E₂!

(r 17) [...] there is ... in a man's house: dilapidation of the house.

r 1818

[... EME].⸢ŠID⸣ 2 KUN-MEŠ-ša₂ ina E₂ NA IGI.IGI ša₂-li-mu IGI KUN-sa TIqe₂

(r 18) (If) a two-tailed lizard keeps appearing in a man's house: well-being. He who saw (it) will take its tail. [...] ... of his house that ... in that (gloss: that) house neither protective deity nor tutelary deity will approach the man's house.

r 1919

[...] x E₂-šu₂ ša₂ x x ina E₂ šu-a-tu₄ša₂-tu₄ dALAD u dLAMMA ina E₂ NA NU TE

r 2020

[...] ša₂ 2 KUN-MEŠ-ša₂ ⸢KUN-ša₃!⸣ ša₂ ZA₃ GID₂.DA ina E₂ NA IGI DINGIR-MEŠ ze-nu-tu₄ ana E₂ NA GUR-MEŠ-ni

(r 20) (If) a two-tailed [...], whose right tail is long, is seen in a man's house: angry gods will return to the man's house.

r 2121

[* EME].⸢ŠID⸣ ša₂ 2 KUN-MEŠ-ša₂ x ⸢KAL?⸣ GI MUŠ GAL₂ši ša₂ GUB₃ GID₂.DA ša₂ ZA₃ LUGUD₂.DA

(r 21) (If) there is a two-tailed lizard ... a snake ... (and) its (left) tail is long (whereas) its right (tail) is short [...] wrath will fall on the man's house; there will be distress on the man's son, but that house will reveal riches.

r 2222

[...] ⸢DINGIR.ŠA₃⸣.DIB.BA ana E₂ NA ŠUB-MEŠ ⸢ina⸣ IBILA NA ud-du-u₂ GAL₂-ma NIG₂.TUK ⸢E₂⸣ BI ⸢u₂?⸣-kal-lam

r 2323

* EME.ŠID ša₂ 2 KUN-MEŠ-ša₂ ana UGU ⸢NA⸣ ŠUBut NA BI UGU EN DU₁₁-šu₂ GUBaz

(r 23) (If) a two-tailed lizard falls on top of a man: that man will triumph over his enemy.

r 2424

* EME.ŠID ša₂ 2 KUN-MEŠ-ša₂ ana UGU ⸢NA⸣ ŠUBut NA BI KI.GUB-šu₂ IGI

(r 24) (If) a two-tailed lizard falls on top of a man: that man will find his position.

r 2525

* EME.ŠID ša₂ 2 KUN-MEŠ-ša₂ ina E₂ NA ⸢IGI⸣ KUN!(GAL₂-)sa KU₅is [...] U₈ mu-še-niq-ti KEŠ₂

(r 25) (If) a two-tailed lizard is seen in a man's house: you will cut its tail (and) you will bind (it) to the [...] of a suckling ewe. If that ewe rejects its lamb: that sign is bad. If that ewe loves its lamb: that sign is good.

r 2626

šum₄-ma U₈ ši-i SILA₄-sa i-zi-ir GISKIM ⸢ši⸣-[i] ⸢ḫa⸣-ṭa-at

r 2727

šum₄-ma U₈ ši-i SILA₄-sa i-ram ⸢GISKIM ši⸣-i dam-qat

r 2828

* EME.ŠID ki-ma ŠU.SAR pat-lu-ma TA giš⸢GIŠIMMAR ŠUBtu₂⸣-ni ŠUBti [...]

(r 28) (If) lizards are entwined like string and fall from a date palm: epidemic (of) [...].

r 2929

* EME.ŠID ša₂ 2 KUN-MEŠ-ša₂ ina E₂ NA IGI INIM la₃ DU₁₀ ina E₂ ⸢NA⸣ [...]

(r 29) (If) a two-tailed lizard is seen in a man's house: unfriendly words; in the man's house [...].

r 3030

* EME.ŠID ina E₂ NA mim-ma TIqe₂ qi-bi la₃ SILIM.MA ⸢U₃⸣ [...]

(r 30) (If) a lizard takes something in a man's house: [...] unfavourable predictions.

r 3131

* EME.ŠID nu-up-pu-ul-tu₄ ina E₂ NA GAL₂ [...] x [...]

(r 31) (If) there is a one-eyed lizard in a man's house [...] ... [...].

r 3232

* EME.ŠID ina tal!-⸢lak⸣-ti E₂ NA U₃ [...]

(r 32) (If) a lizard, in the access way of a man's house ... [...].

r 3333

* EME.ŠID ina x x x [...] x [...] ḪI x [...]

(r 33) (If) a lizard, in ... [...] ... [...] ... [...].

r 3434

* EME.ŠID ina x [...] ⸢U₂⸣ TU ḪI x [...]

(r 34) (If) a lizard, in ... [...] ... [...].

r 3535

[*] ⸢EME⸣.ŠID ina E₂ [NA] ma-gal ⸢ŠUB-MEŠ⸣ x ŠUB E₂

(r 35) (If) lizards fall in large amounts in a man's house ...: abandoning of the house.

r 3636

[* EME].⸢ŠID⸣ ina ⸢KI⸣.TAnu I.DIB E₂ NA U₃.TU ŠUB E₂

(r 36) (If) a lizard gives birth beneath the threshold of a man's house: abandoning of the house.

r 3737

[* EME].⸢ŠID⸣ ina ⸢KI⸣.TAnu na₄UR₅.UR₅ ⸢U₃⸣.TU EN E₂ ina ŠU x [...]

(r 37) (If) a lizard gives birth beneath the grindstone: the house's owner will [...] in the hand [...].

r 3838

[...] uruduŠEN MUNUS U₃.TU ⸢MUNUS⸣ BI MAŠ.TAB.BA [...]

(r 38) [...] gives birth (in) a woman's cauldron: that woman [...] twins.

r 3939

[...] uruduŠEN MUNUS KU₄ ⸢MUNUS⸣ ši ŠA₃ [...]

(r 39) [...] enters a woman's cauldron: that woman [...] heart [...].

r 4040

[...] [dug]ŠAB.I₃ ŠUB EN E₂ UŠ₂ ŠU x [...]

(r 40) [...] falls (in) a bowl (for oil): the owner of that house will die ... [...].

r 4141

[...] MUNUS KU₄ MUNUS BI KI x [...]

(r 41) [...] enters a woman's [...]: that woman ... [...].

(colophon)
r 4242

[...] ⸢TA⸣ ŠA₃ DUB.GAL⸢li⸣ GABA.RI ⸢TIN⸣.[TIRki ...]

(r 42) [...] from a big tablet, Babylonian copy [...].
